web_results.title: What to Expect from Xi Jinping Regarding Taiwan in 2026? | World News - The Times of India
web_results.description: Will 2026 become the year when Chinese President Xi Jinping decides that the long-promised “reunification” of Taiwan can no longer wait?Driving the newsChina’s latest round of live-fire military drills in the air and seas around Taiwan landed with unusually sharp timing: just as the calendar flipped another year closer to 2027, a date that looms larger for US defense planners than almost any other. ‘3, 2, 1, FIRE!’: China Rockets ROAR On Day 2 Of Taiwan War Games Yet Trump Says He Isn't 'WORRIED' Beijing described the exercises as a “stern warning” to separatist forces.

web_results.description: To trade on "Will China invade Taiwan by end of 2026?," simply choose whether you believe the answer is "Yes" or "No." Each side has a current price that reflects the market's implied probability. Enter your amount and click "Trade." If you buy "Yes" shares and the outcome resolves as "Yes," each share pays out $1.

web_results.description: By Sub-Lieutenant Joseph T. Reilly, Royal Navy · If China were to begin a war against Taiwan as soon as 2027 and the United States decides to mount a regional response, the United States would lose.1

web_results.description: Now I sit in the year of our lord 2026 thinking about how that mentality is leading us down a road of active conflicts on a scale not seen since the last fires were put out in Japan in 1945. ... But yeah, obviously don't just fully trust one dude on the future of geopolitics as gospel. ... Eh, this is more of a simple logistics issue. Invading a nation like Taiwan from the ocean is a massive undertaking.

news_results.title: Thanks to Trump, Xi Has Time on His Side With Taiwan
news_results.description: Beijing is less likely to risk an invasion while Trump is facilitating its pressure campaign against Taipei.
